title: baccharoides adoensis var. mossambiquensis
intro: Baccharoides adoensis var. mossambiquensis, also known as East African wormwood or African wormwood, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimalarial, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Baccharoides adoensis var. mossambiquensis include relief from fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and fight off infections. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are typically dried and used as a tea or tincture, and it can also be used as a topical application for skin conditions and wounds.
